rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections in the input AADL model
Ensure that AGREE annex blocks are inserted in the correct scope to maintain the AADL architecture
Preserve the existing AADL structure and relationships when inserting AGREE annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use the correct syntax for AGREE annex blocks: `annex agree {** ... **};`
Ensure that the inserted AGREE annex blocks are valid and follow the AGREE syntax rules
anti_patterns: Avoid inserting AGREE annex blocks in invalid scopes, such as outside of component type or implementation definitions
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
